pattern: Binary search on square half-side or sort points by Chebyshev distance; greedily expand the square until a tag collision would occur.
hints: A point (x,y) is inside a square of half-side L centered at origin iff max(|x|,|y|) <= L. Sort points by this Chebyshev distance.
Binary search on the half-side L: count how many distinct tags appear when all points with Chebyshev distance <= L are included, checking if any tag appears more than once.

Process points in non-decreasing order of Chebyshev distance. Expand the square one 'distance level' at a time. As soon as adding the next level introduces a duplicate tag, stop and return the current count.

edge_inputs.description: example 3: first two share same tag at L=2, answer 0
two points at same distance with same tag - must not include either
two points same tag but different distances - include only the closer one
point at origin counts as distance 0 inside any non-degenerate square
26 points all different tags at same Chebyshev distance

stress.description: random points with random tags from small alphabet
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    points = [[rng.randint(-100, 100), rng.randint(-100, 100)] for _ in range(n)]
    # deduplicate points
    seen = set()
    unique = []
    for p in points:
        key = (p[0], p[1])
        if key not in seen:
            seen.add(key)
            unique.append(p)
    points = unique[:n]
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghij') for _ in range(len(points)))
    return [points, s]
